title: IELTS Academic Mock Test 1
description: Complete IELTS Academic test with all 4 skills

words.definition: To become aware of through the senses
words.example: Fish perceive electrical signals in water.
The fish responds to electrical stimuli.

words.definition: A material that allows electricity to pass through
words.example: Water is an efficient conductor of electricity.

words.definition: An animal that hunts other animals for food
words.example: Sharks are top predators in the ocean.
The jamming avoidance response is a fascinating mechanism.
As the shark reaches proximity to its prey, it attacks.

passages.title: Electroreception
content: A Open your eyes in sea water and it is difficult to see much more than a murky, bleary green colour. Sounds, too, are garbled and difficult to comprehend. Without specialised equipment humans would be lost in these deep sea habitats, so how do fish make it seem so easy? Much of this is due to a biological phenomenon known as electroreception – the ability to perceive and act upon electrical stimuli as part of the overall senses. This ability is only found in aquatic or amphibian species because water is an efficient conductor of electricity.

B Electroreception comes in two variants. While all animals (including humans) generate electric signals, because they are emitted by the nervous system, some animals have the ability – known as passive electroreception – to receive and decode electric signals generated by other animals in order to sense their location.

C Other creatures can go further still. However, animals with active electroreception possess bodily organs that generate special electric signals on cue. These can be used for mating signals and territorial displays as well as locating objects in the water.

subTasks.instruction: Which paragraph contains the following information?

questions.text: how electroreception can be used to help fish reproduce
a possible use for electroreception that will benefit humans

subTasks.instruction: Complete the summary. NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.text: Electroreception is found mainly in ___ creatures.

sections.title: Task 1: Data Description
sections.instruction: Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.instruction: Write about the following topic. Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your knowledge or experience.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

sections.title: Section 1: Conversation
sections.transcript:
Man: Hello, I'm looking for information about the university library...
Woman: Of course, I'd be happy to help. What would you like to know?

questions.text: What time does the library open?

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction and Interview
parts.instruction: Answer the examiner's questions about familiar topics.

questions.text: What kind of place is it?
What's the most interesting part of your town/village?
Would you say it's a good place to live? (Why?)
Tell me about the kind of accommodation you live in.
How long have you lived there?

parts.title: Part 2: Long Turn
parts.instruction: You will have 1 minute to prepare, then speak for 1-2 minutes on the topic.

topicCard.title: Describe a skill you learned when you were a child.
cueCards: And explain why this skill was useful to you

parts.title: Part 3: Discussion
parts.instruction: Discuss more abstract related topics with the examiner.

questions.text: What skills do you think are important for children to learn?
Do you think children learn better from teachers or from their parents?
